Simple image with npm logo and the number 12npm 12 shipped this week, and I have been looking forward to it. Plenty of people I know moved over to Yarn or pnpm years ago, but unless a project gave me a specific reason to switch I have stuck with npm, on the theory that the package manager from the people who run npmjs.com itself wouldn’t steer me wrong. This release feels like that loyalty paying off. For the first time since npm existed, running npm install does not hand arbitrary code execution to every package in your dependency tree.
If you have been anywhere near the JavaScript ecosystem over the last year, you will know why that matters. We have had Nx s1ngularity, the Shai-Hulud worm, and a hijacked Axios maintainer account, among plenty of others. Different attackers, different payloads, and nearly all of them leaned on the same weakness: npm would run whatever a package asked it to, the moment you installed it.
In this post I’ll cover the following changes:

• Install scripts are now off by default

• Git and remote URL dependencies are blocked too

• The changes coming to publishing
1. Install scripts are now off by default
Historically, any package anywhere in your dependency tree could define preinstall, install, or postinstall…
